anchor_text: After an accident, Neo, a lonely man in his 40s, loses his eyesight. He learns to handle the new challenges the world now has for him. He makes a new friend, Anna, who is very willing to help him out, finding connections that he never thought possible before.
text_a: Adam has a large circle of friends. One day, he loses his hearing in an accident. He struggles with the new challenges in communication, alienating his friends, and finally becomes very lonely.
text_b: Brian is a London-based artist who creates street art. He leads a lonely life. One day, he encounters a fellow artist who is painting what looks to be an identical picture to the one he is working on. The two make a deep connection over their shared love for art and remain friends to this day.

contra_reason.theme: Story A and the Anchor share a specific abstract theme of coping with a newly acquired disability after an accident. Story B shares a broader theme of finding meaningful connection and friendship.
contra_reason.course: Story B mirrors the Anchor's course of action: a lonely individual encounters a new person, develops a deep connection, and forms a lasting friendship. Story A follows an opposite trajectory where a social person loses a sense and subsequently loses their social circle.
contra_reason.outcomes: Story B and the Anchor result in a positive outcome of companionship and connection. Story A results in an outcome of total isolation and loneliness, which is the polar opposite of the Anchor's conclusion.

result.final_explanation: While Story A and the Anchor share a common theme (disability), their courses of action and outcomes are essentially opposites. Story B is more similar because its structural progression and final outcome of friendship match the Anchor.

anchor_text: Two travelers, Eli and Stan, in medieval Europe are stopped by a roadblock. A group of robbers descends upon them. The two defend themselves. The robbers capture Stan. Eli is forced to make a decision: flee the scene or give up and stay with Stan. He surrenders, and the robbers take their belongings but let the two live.
text_a: A small group of travelers is heading west by train in 1800s USA. At night, the train stops, and as passengers peer out the windows, they spot hoards of zombies. The group makes their way out, fighting off multiple zombie attacks as they go. It is only as they leave the train that they notice one of their own, Dan, is missing. Deciding not to leave Dan behind, they perform a daring rescue, saving him just before the zombies close in on him and still make it out. In a multi-day effort, the group manages to lose zombies and make it out unharmed.
text_b: Two travelers, Eli and Stan, make their way from their home village to the kingdom's capital. The roads are dangerous. The two hear countless tales of robbers, but sticking to the main roads and only traveling by daylight, they make it to their destination and are able to explore the kingdom's center of power for themselves.

contra_reason.theme: Story A and the Anchor share the abstract theme of travelers encountering and surpassing dangerous difficulties through a selfless choice to save a companion [cite: 379-380].
contra_reason.course: Story A mirrors the Anchor's progression: travelers are stopped, an attack occurs, a member of the party is lost/captured, and the remaining members choose to risk themselves [cite: 365-368].
contra_reason.outcomes: Story A and the Anchor both conclude with the travelers surviving the ordeal and remaining together .

result.final_explanation: Story A is more similar because it shares the same core narrative of travelers facing an attack and choosing to help one another, whereas Story B only shares superficial names and settings.

anchor_text: In 1836 in southern California... American settlers and the U.S. government discriminate against the Mexican landowners and frequently take their land by force... Wealthy Latino ranchers... decide to misdirect a U.S. government ship carrying gold... The hero is the now-estranged adoptive son Finley... who tries to prevent this while still saving his family.
text_a: Joaquin Murieta goes to California with the hope of finding gold there. On the way, he meets a beautiful girl whom he marries, but their happiness was short-lived. Suddenly his wife dies.
text_b: Timothy Benson, a young intellectual and greenhorn inherits a gold mine. Rodrigo Rodriguez, a bandido leader will stop at nothing to get Timothy to deed him the mine. It is up to Timothy's foreman and his friends to teach Timothy to be a tough hombre and with their help, stop the bandit, Rodrigo Rodriguez.

anchor_text: Alice and Bob get married. They buy a farm and raise wonderful children.
text_a: Harry and Louise found a business together. Investing much effort, they lead it to success over the years.
text_b: Alice and Bob are neighbors. After pleasantly chatting every now and then, in a turn of events, they start a fight about the tree growing on their property line.

anchor_text: Andrew goes to the shop to buy food and drinks. He then heads home and prepares everything for his family’s arrival. As aunts and uncles arrive, he can impress them with homemade cookies and fancy drinks.
text_a: Zoie buys ammunition and guns; she will need them. Back home, she prepares well, setting up traps and protected firing positions. When the Zombies rush her doors, she is prepared and can deal out destruction. Nonetheless, she cannot win against the unending hoards of undead.
text_b: Erica is great at building paper planes. One day, to her surprise, she attends a competition, and despite little preparation, she wins!

anchor_text: Maven is a magician; ever since finishing his apprenticeship, he has worked on developing novel magic in his tower. As the years go on, he gets fewer and fewer visitors, focusing his life only on his work. After he dies alone in his tower, nobody finds his body for many decades.
text_a: The expedition is not going as planned. Some party members have abandoned the mission and tried to return home. One day, the other three remaining members decide to head home, and Ellie realizes she is the last one in the expedition party. Working through snow and ice, she underestimates the storm and freezes to death; nobody ever finds her.
text_b: Three friends go on a fishing trip. They catch nothing. They still had a great time.

contra_reason.outcomes: Story A and the Anchor share a highly similar outcome: the protagonist dies alone and their body remains undiscovered for a long period. Story B has a positive outcome where the characters enjoy themselves despite failing their objective.

result.final_explanation: Neither option presents a similar course of action or theme, but Story A is selected because it shares the specific outcome of dying alone and being forgotten with the Anchor.

anchor_text: In the trenches of World War I, Greg is hit by a grenade splinter. He is in tremendous pain, but his comrades manage to evacuate him from the position. After spending weeks fighting the infection in his leg, he succumbs to his injuries.
text_a: Jill was driving home when another car suddenly crashed into hers. After receiving medical attention, she recovered within just days and now advocates for traffic safety.
text_b: As Major Miller gives the command to charge, he is not sure if his men can manage it. In a heroic effort, they capture the next position. Only one day later, though, it is again lost to the enemy.

Story B, by contrast, focuses on military strategy and territorial control, sharing only a superficial concrete setting (war) with the Anchor, which does not contribute to narrative similarity.

contra_reason.outcomes: Neither story shares a similar outcome with the Anchor. The Anchor ends in death, Story A ends in recovery and advocacy, and Story B ends in a lost military position.

anchor_text: Anna loses her purse. She retraces her steps but cannot find it. Dan finds it and helpfully returns it to her.
text_a: Brian lost his backpack. He was terrified because there were important documents in it. After an hour of intense search he finally found it.
text_b: Alex loses his engagement ring while swimming. He freaks out, and after hours of diving for it, he still cannot find it.

contra_reason.course: Story A follows a similar sequence to the Anchor: losing an item, searching for it, and the search concluding with the item being found. Story B follows the Anchor only through the loss and the failed search but lacks the final structural step of discovery.
contra_reason.outcomes: Story A results in a successful recovery of the property, aligning with the Anchor's positive resolution. Story B results in a permanent loss, which is the opposite outcome of the Anchor.

result.final_explanation: Story A is more similar because all three aspects match with the Anchor whereas Story B only matches in the abstract theme.
